auth_notes: Uses Basic Authentication requiring an API Key and API Secret generated in the ShipStation dashboard (Settings > API Settings). In n8n, use the 'Basic Auth' credentials type with the Key as the username and Secret as the password.
gotchas: The rate limit is relatively low (40 requests/min), which may require n8n Wait nodes or Split In Batches for high-volume stores.
Pagination uses 'page' and 'pageSize' parameters which must be manually iterated using a loop in n8n.
Webhooks must be registered via the API or manually in the ShipStation UI to point to the n8n Webhook node URL.
research_notes: The integration will rely entirely on the HTTP Request node. The base URL is https://ssapi.shipstation.com. Since ShipStation is owned by the same company as ShipEngine (which has a native node), users might find ShipEngine a more 'plug-and-play' alternative if they are flexible on the platform, though ShipStation offers a more robust UI for end-users.
